name: Statuspage
description: Create and manage public or private status pages for communicating service health to users. Manage components, report incidents, schedule maintenances, and notify subscribers via email, SMS, Slack, or webhooks. Ideal for agents that need to report outages, update component statuses, or automate incident communication.

capabilities.name: manage_incidents
capabilities.description: Create, update, and resolve incidents on a status page. Set impact level, affected components, and status (investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved). Supports scheduled maintenances and notification delivery control.

capabilities.name: manage_components
capabilities.description: Create, list, update, and delete components on a status page. Set component status to operational, degraded_performance, partial_outage, major_outage, or under_maintenance. Organize components into groups.

capabilities.name: get_status_summary
capabilities.description: Retrieve the overall status summary of a page including all component statuses, active incidents, and scheduled maintenances. Returns a rollup indicator (none, minor, major, critical) for quick health checks.

capabilities.name: manage_subscribers
capabilities.description: Add, list, and remove subscribers who receive incident notifications. Supports email, SMS, Slack, Microsoft Teams, and webhook subscriber types. Subscribers can be scoped to specific components.
